titlestmt.titleproper: Collection of Identification Photographs of Industrial Workers of the World Strikers
Identification photographs of Industrial Workers of the World strikers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday March 13, 2020 at 04:23

did.unittitle: Collection of identification photographs of Industrial Workers of the World strikers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: 60 identification photographs of 59 individuals arrested in connection with the Everett Massacre, an armed confrontation in Everett, Washington, between local authorities and members of the Industrial Workers of the World union (also referred to as "Wobblies") who had come from Seattle in support of striking shingle workers, on 1916 November 5. Identifications are stamped on the versos and completed in manuscript.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Michael Vinson on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2014.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged numerically by identification number assigned to each individual.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Collection of Identification Photographs of Industrial Workers of the World Strikers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Information listed in the finding aid taken from versos of identification photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 60 identification photographs of 59 individuals arrested in connection with the Everett Massacre, an armed confrontation in Everett, Washington, between local authorities and members of the Industrial Workers of the World union (also referred to as "Wobblies") who had come from Seattle in support of striking shingle workers, on 1916 November 5. Identifications are stamped on the versos and completed in manuscript.

Listed information includes identification number, name, alias, crime, age, height, weight, build, hair, eyes, complexion, mustache, location of birth, occupation, date of arrest, date received, date of sentence, and sentence. Also included are remarks.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Collection of Identification Photographs of Industrial Workers of the World Strikers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: No. 397. Frank E. Smith (born circa 1883, Manchester, New Hampshire), laborer. Arrested for carrying a concealed weapon (blackjack)
No. 4797. C. Givynn (born circa 1881, Missouri), longshoreman. Arrested for gross misdemeanor; identified member of the Industrial Workers of the World
No. 4826 John Patterson (born circa 1885, Illinois), painter. Arrested for vagrancy; identified member of the Industrial Workers of the World, was in charge of IWW hall after Verona Battle
No. 4828 Harry Feinberg (born circa 1891, Illinois), cleaner and dryer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4830 James Bradley (born circa 1870, Minnesota), miner. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4832 Adolph Ericsson (born circa 1890, Sweden), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4835 John Rawlings (born circa 1890, Wisconsin), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4836 James D. Bates (born circa 1887, Illinois), steamfitters. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4838 Charles Adams (born circa 1888, Illinois), also known as J. P. McDowell, printer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4841 George Murphy (born circa 1888, Kentucky), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4842 Harold Miller (born circa 1895, Kansas), gas fitter. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4843 John "Red" Downs (born circa 1888, Colorado), sailor. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4844 C.P. McLennan (born circa 1868, Georgia), longshoreman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4846 H. A. Cheethaw (born circa 1891, Florida), carpenter. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4847 Hartson Peters (born circa 1884, Virginia), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4849 Frank Stewart (born circa 1881, Canada), logger. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4850 E.M. Beck (born circa 1861, New York), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4852 Robert Leonard (born circa 1892, Michigan), farmer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4853 W.H. Brown (born circa 1876, Maryland), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4854 Albert Shrene (born circa 1876, Illinois), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4856 William Coffin (born circa 1888, California), motorman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4857 Charles "Austin" Auspor (born circa 1883, Wisconsin), teamster. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4858 Charles "7" Berg (born circa 1896, Germany), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4859 Malachai O'Neill (born circa 1877, Ireland), blacksmith. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4860 Thomas Tracy (born circa 1886, Pennsylvania), cranesman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4861 William Davis (born circa 1881, Maryland), cook. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4862 Oscar Johnson (born circa 1892, Sweden), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4864 William Winn (born circa 1872, Maryland), miner. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4865 Charles "Cowboy" H. Cody (born circa 1870, Montana), painter. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4866 Thomas H. Tracy (born circa 1880, Nebraska), also known as Gio Martin, teamster. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4867 A.B. Wimborne (born circa 1894, England), busboy. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4871 Everett Truett (born circa 1888, Pennsylvania), longshoreman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4872 John Ross (born circa 1880, Massachusetts), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4873 James Kelly (born circa 1880, New York), also known as Whiteford Willis, cook. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4874 Jack Patterson (born circa 1892, Illinois), also known as Thomas Williams, laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4875 Pat Lyons (born circa 1870, England), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4876 John Nugent (born circa 1878, New York), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4877 William Lawson (born circa 1884, Washington), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4878 Victor Johnson (born circa 1879, Finland), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4879 James Powers (born circa 1870, Massachusetts), sheet metal worker. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4881 Earl Osborne (born circa 1884, North Carolina), logger. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4884 Roy Davis (born circa 1869, California), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4885 E.F. Hollingsworth (born circa 1887, North Carolina), fireman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4886 Michael J. Riley (born circa 1893, New York), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4887 Clarence "Paddy the Pig" Cyphert (born circa 1881, Washington), logger. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4888 Jim Mack (born circa 1885, Ireland), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4889 Joseph Manning (born circa 1888, Pennsylvania), auto repairer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4890 Fred "Frank" Cupler (born circa 1890, Canada), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4891 D. Stevens (born circa 1895, Canada), longshoreman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4892 Carl Newman (born circa 1886, Sweden), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4894 Charles Black (born circa 1893, Pennsylvania), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4896 William Shay (born circa 1888, Massachusetts), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4897 Frank Boyd (born circa 1973, Illinois), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4900 Lewis Morgan (born circa 1893, Texas), also known as Dan McCall, laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4902 H. Sobol (born circa 1890, Russia), laborer. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4913 Ed Roth (born circa 1885), longshoreman. Arrested for murder 1.
No. 4914 J.H. Beyer (born circa 1860, Michigan), painter. Arrested for murder 1.
Oleg Blackmon (born circa 1895, Hamilton, Ontario), organizer and lecturer. I.W.W. member.
John Furham, I.W.W. member, ran out of Yakima, Arrested at Hood River, Concealed Weapon
